title: castilleja galactionovae
intro: Castilleja galactionovae, also known as mountain Indian pipe, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. It belongs to the borage family and is recognized by its unique, yellowish-green flowers and distinctive shape. Castilleja galactionovae has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, with potential health benefits including reduced pain and inflammation, improved wound healing, and enhanced immune function. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, with some herbalists also using it in salves and ointments to leverage its medicinal properties.
